title: Alnus multinervis
intro: Alnus multinervis, also known as the many-veined alder, is a species of deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of northern China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Alnus multinervis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Alnus multinervis is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being incorporated into herbal teas and tinctures.
taxonomy: Alnus multinervis is a species of tree in the family Betulaceae. It is native to China and belongs to the genus Alnus, which includes other species of alder trees. The family Betulaceae is a group of flowering plants that also includes beech and birch trees. Alnus multinervis is a deciduous tree that grows in the wild and is known for its distinctive leaves. The classification of Alnus multinervis reflects its evolutionary relationships to other plants in the Betulaceae family.
uses: Alnus multinervis, also known as the many-veined alder, is a plant species native to China and other parts of East Asia. Research on its medicinal uses is limited, but some traditional Chinese medicine sources suggest that the plant has anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health issues. More research is required to fully understand its potential uses and applications in modern medicine.

preparations: Alnus multinervis preparations have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, and in some cultures, they are made into teas or infusions.
